title: The PMM's Hippocratic Oath
subtitle: Responsible GenAI in an era of infinite persuasion

slides.title: The PMM's Hippocratic Oath
slides.subtitle: How to scale AI marketing without destroying your brand's soul.
bullets: The Elephant: AI can write 10,000 lies a second. Who catches them?
The Desire: We want the speed, but we also want to not get fired.
The Goal: Ship stupid fast, but sleep soundly at night.

slides.title: What you're getting today
bullets: The Reality Check (a.k.a brands that fired up AI and immediately regretted it)
Five wildly uncomfortable truths about AI in marketing
A 7-day survival guide so we don't become the next cautionary tale

slides.title: We built a Ferrari engine with bicycle brakes
bullets: AI lets us write 8x more content. Hooray?
Our legal review team is still just Bob and Steve. Not hooray.
The bottleneck isn't creating content anymore. It's knowing if it's true.

slides.title: The Promise: The AI Flywheel
bullets: Drafting: Write the first draft while making coffee
Testing: A/B/C/D/E/F testing without begging design for banners
Enablement: Turn a launch brief into sales scripts in roughly 4 seconds

slides.title: Why we are doing this
bullets: Pre-launch: Actually testing narrative angles instead of just guessing
Launch week: Spitting out assets faster than sales can ask for them
Post-launch: Handling competitor objections before the webinar ends

slides.title: Failure map of infinite persuasion
slides.lead: The rate of unverified claims scales exponentially with output volume, generating massive hidden liability debt.
footnotes: Represents projected compounding error rate without verification gates.
Model assumes standard ChatGPT output parity without rigorous retrieval safeguards.

slides.title: Receipt 1: Deloitte
slides.lead: Deloitte's credibility collapsed after a paid report included completely fabricated citations.
footnotes: Represents highly public failure of unverified generative text rendering.
Auditable evidence posture is now a firm-level mandate.
bullets: Fabricated references in a paid report
Credibility collapsed before trust could recover
Lesson: evidence posture must be auditable

slides.title: Receipt 1 deep dive
bullets: One bad citation propagates through sales narrative
Executive buyer validates and trust alarms spread
Deals stall before technical proof starts

slides.title: Receipt 2: Air Canada
bullets: Incorrect chatbot policy guidance
Brand still held liable by tribunal
Lesson: channel changed, accountability did not

slides.title: Receipt 2 deep dive
bullets: Missing policy classifier gate
Missing legal-approved answer bank
Missing escalation fallback path

slides.title: Receipt 3: Coca-Cola
bullets: Style cues matched, authenticity perception dropped
Audience called output emotionally synthetic
Lesson: voice parity is not value parity

slides.title: Receipt 3 deep dive
bullets: Track voice consistency vs value consistency
Review with lawsuit / regulator / front-page lenses
Trust gap is where brand risk compounds

slides.title: Counter-receipt: licensed-data positioning
bullets: Commercial-safe narrative can reduce trust anxiety
Provenance should be headline-level information
Transferable pattern: truth + clarity + confidence

slides.title: Not bans. Held tensions.
bullets: Keep velocity, add discipline
Codify decisions before publish
Move from policy theater to operational behavior

slides.title: The oath
slides.lead: The mandate is not to ban generative AI, but to institutionalize specific behavioral tensions.
slides.foundation: Core Product Marketing Standard Operating Procedure

pillars.title: Brand Cohesion
items: Sounds like us vs Is us

slides.title: Tension 1: Speed vs verification
slides.lead: A generated claim without a verifiable and reproducible source is a direct liability vector.

matrix.q1: The Goal: Rapid, auditable generation via strict templates
matrix.q2: The Trap: Slow manual writing disguised as 'safety'
matrix.q3: Legacy Process: Slow manual writing with poor QA
matrix.q4: The Danger Zone: Raw ChatGPT output pasted into market

slides.title: Claim Card snapshot
bullets: Fields: source, scope, reproducibility, harm tier
Attach on battlecards, datasheets, launch narratives
Escalate when harm tier is medium/high

slides.title: Example Claim Card
slides.lead: Every high-impact claim must be attached to a verifiable, reproducible card before publication.

card.claim: Our platform reduces customer churn by 34% within the first 90 days of deployment.
card.verdict: ⚠ Requires Legal Review Before External Use

slides.title: Tension 2: Personalization vs manipulation
bullets: Do not optimize for anxiety exploitation
Design prompts that reduce confusion
Apply Benefit Alignment Test before publish

slides.title: Tension 3: Efficiency vs stewardship
bullets: Brute-force prompting inflates cost and review debt
Use smallest model that delivers required quality
Diff-based review improves throughput and trust

slides.title: Tension 4: Sounds like us vs is us
bullets: Avoid autonomy over-claims and certainty theater
Rewrite to bounded, testable, defensible language
Brand Signature Test: lawsuit/regulator/front-page

slides.title: Tension 5a: Who converts vs who gets erased
bullets: Default outputs over-represent majority personas
Require explicit edge-case coverage in prompts
Flag product limitations by segment honestly

slides.title: Tension 5b: Style laundering
bullets: Avoid named imitation of living creators
Use style cards: tone, structure, devices, constraints
License, attribute, or translate into explicit constraints

slides.title: Decision architecture
slides.lead: Escalation paths must be mapped against capability maturity before a crisis occurs.

slides.title: Toolkit + 7-day challenge
bullets: Pilot one launch asset this week
Run all five gates before publish
Ship an internal retrospective by day seven

slides.title: Close + Q&A
slides.subtitle: Where does this break in your org?
bullets: What is your highest-risk claim in market now?
Where does verification fail in your workflow?
What can you pilot by next week?
